pi_items.source: Successfully accessed; confirmed valid with downloadable files (BBBC005_v1_images.zip, BBBC005_v1_ground_truth.zip)
Official BBBC005 page states 'Each image is 696 x 520 pixels in 8-bit TIF format'
Downloaded and analyzed BBBC005_v1_ground_truth.zip ground truth images using PIL/Image library. Image mode 'L' = 8-bit grayscale. Official BBBC005 documentation states '8-bit TIF format'

BBBC005_results_bray.csv downloaded from https://data.broadinstitute.org/bbbc/BBBC005/BBBC005_results_bray.csv. Image_Metadata_ActualCellCount column shows 400 images with 1 cell (minimum). Also confirmed in official documentation: 'cell count is 1-100'
BBBC005_results_bray.csv shows 400 images with 100 cells (maximum). Official BBBC005 page states 'number of cells simulated in the image ranges from 1-100'

Calculated average cell area per cell: (total_cell_pixels_from_ground_truth) / (cell_count_from_metadata). Analyzed 600 matched images. Actual range observed: 748-794 pixels per cell. Metadata range [200-3000] contains actual data.
Same analysis as expected_avg_area_min. Actual maximum observed: 794 pixels per cell. Mean: 769.2 pixels. Metadata range [200-3000] contains actual data.

No authoritative documentation found on BBBC005 circularity specifications. Circularity is not listed as a standard output in BBBC005 ground truth. Would only be computed from actual cell segmentation methods, which vary.
No authoritative documentation found on BBBC005 circularity specifications. Standard range for circularity is [0,1] by definition, where 1 is perfectly circular.
This is a parameter for the ImageJ/Fiji 'Analyze Particles' function specified in the task description, not a documented characteristic of BBBC005 dataset
This is a parameter for the ImageJ/Fiji 'Analyze Particles' function specified in the task description, not a documented characteristic of BBBC005 dataset

privileged_info_summary: BBBC005 is a real, publicly-available dataset from the Broad Bioimage Benchmark Collection containing 9,600 synthetic cell images. Images are 696×520 pixels in 8-BIT (not 16-bit) TIF format. Cell counts range from 1-100 cells per image (not 10-200 as claimed), with documented counts in BBBC005_results_bray.csv. Average cell areas range from approximately 748-794 pixels per cell, which falls within the claimed range of 200-3000 pixels. Images were generated using the SIMCEP simulator with 25% clustering probability. Circularity values are unverified and not documented in official BBBC005 specifications.
